title: Cache Lookup Algorithm
description: Demonstrates how CPU accesses data in cache hierarchy
code:
FUNCTION CacheAccess(address):
 // Try L1 cache first
 IF L1Lookup(address) == HIT:
 RETURN L1Data
 // L1 miss, try L2
 ELSE IF L2Lookup(address) == HIT:
 MoveDataL2toL1(address)
 RETURN L2Data
 // L2 miss, fetch from main memory
 ELSE:
 LoadBlockFromMemory(address)
 PlaceInL1Cache(address)
 PlaceInL2Cache(address)
 RETURN MemoryData

FUNCTION L1Lookup(address):
 SetIndex = address[IndexBits]
 Line = L1[SetIndex]
 IF Line.Valid AND Line.Tag == address[TagBits]:
 RETURN HIT
 ELSE:
 RETURN MISS
explanation: Shows hierarchical cache lookup process. On L1 hit, returns immediately. On L1 miss, checks L2. On L2 miss, fetches from memory and populates all cache levels.

complexity.time: O(1) for hit, O(100+) for miss

title: LRU Cache Replacement Algorithm
description: Implements Least Recently Used replacement for cache lines
code:
FUNCTION LRUReplacement(cacheSet, newBlock):
 // Find LRU line (least recently used)
 minLRU = INFINITY
 lruLineIndex = -1
 
 FOR i = 0 TO SetAssociativity - 1:
 IF cacheSet[i].LRUCounter < minLRU:
 minLRU = cacheSet[i].LRUCounter
 lruLineIndex = i
 
 // Evict LRU line if dirty
 IF cacheSet[lruLineIndex].Dirty == TRUE:
 WriteBackToMemory(cacheSet[lruLineIndex])
 
 // Insert new block
 cacheSet[lruLineIndex] = newBlock
 cacheSet[lruLineIndex].LRUCounter = 0
 cacheSet[lruLineIndex].Dirty = FALSE
 
 // Update LRU counters for other lines
 FOR i = 0 TO SetAssociativity - 1:
 IF i != lruLineIndex:
 cacheSet[i].LRUCounter = cacheSet[i].LRUCounter + 1
 
 RETURN lruLineIndex
explanation: LRU algorithm tracks usage of each cache line using counters. On replacement, evicts line with lowest counter (least recently used), writes back if modified (dirty), then inserts new block and increments other counters.

title: Write-Through Cache Write
description: Demonstrates write-through policy with write buffer
code:
FUNCTION WriteThrough(address, data):
 // Write to cache
 cacheLine = FindCacheLine(address)
 cacheLine.Data = data
 cacheLine.Dirty = FALSE // Not dirty in write-through
 
 // Add to write buffer for main memory
 WriteBuffer.Add(address, data)
 
 // Non-blocking: continue if buffer not full
 IF WriteBuffer.NotFull():
 RETURN // CPU can continue
 ELSE:
 // Wait for buffer space
 WriteBuffer.WaitForSpace()
 
FUNCTION WriteBuffer.Process():
 WHILE WriteBuffer.NotEmpty():
 entry = WriteBuffer.Peek()
 WriteToMainMemory(entry.address, entry.data)
 WriteBuffer.Remove()
explanation: Write-through writes to cache and queues write to memory in buffer. CPU continues if buffer has space, avoiding memory write latency. Main memory gets updated asynchronously.

complexity.time: O(1) to cache, O(buffer) to memory
